proposal_rationale: The M/M/1 Single-Server Queue blueprint is foundationally a queueing-theory model. The canonical top-level taxonomy (Healthcare, Manufacturing, Transportation, etc.) is industry-based and does not have a category for queueing theory as a cross-cutting subject. Proposing 'Queueing Theory' as a non-canonical cross-cutting theme that queueing-focused blueprints can use alongside their Foundational Patterns tag. Future related blueprints (M/M/c, M/G/1, G/G/1, Jackson Network) would share this domain tag.

The M/M/1 blueprint is primarily pedagogical — its scenarios teach concepts (queueing behavior, Little's Law, Pollaczek-Khinchine insight) rather than supporting specific operational decisions. The walkthrough flagged all four scenarios with flavor='pedagogical'. Proposing 'Pedagogical Models' as a non-canonical cross-cutting theme for teaching-oriented blueprints. Future foundational-pattern entries (other queueing models, job shop, inventory policies) would also qualify for this domain.
